skill.name: xcode-project-setup
skill.description: Safely modifies Xcode projects (.pbxproj) to add Swift Packages and link files. Use this skill whenever an iOS project needs dependencies installed (e.g. Firebase, Alamofire).
skill.summary: Automates Xcode project configuration by adding Swift Package Manager dependencies and linking Firebase config files using a native Swift tool.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Firebase skill for safely modifying Xcode project files. The static analyzer flagged 56 potential issues, but all are false positives after manual review. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate open-source project repos (XcodeProj, PathKit, Spectre, AEXML, Firebase). 'C2 keywords' triggered on a git revision hash. 'Weak crypto' matches on `swift-tools-version: 5.9` and version numbers. Shell patterns are Xcode build-phase configurations and markdown code formatting, not arbitrary code execution. The Swift script uses the well-known XcodeProj library API with typed arguments; no command injection risk exists.

low_findings.title: Shell script build phase injected into project
low_findings.description: The script creates a PBXShellScriptBuildPhase for Crashlytics with a hardcoded shell path and script. While the script content is hardcoded (no injection vector), it does add an auto-executing shell phase to the user's Xcode project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed presence of PBXShellScriptBuildPhase construction, but the shellScript string is a hardcoded literal with no user input - no injection risk.

content.user_title: Set Up Xcode Projects and Add Swift Package Dependencies
content.value_statement: Manually editing Xcode .pbxproj files to add Swift packages is error-prone and risks corrupting the project. This skill uses a native Swift tool to safely add SPM dependencies, link products, and configure Firebase plist files without manual file editing.
actual_capabilities: Adds Swift Package Manager dependencies to Xcode projects via command line
Links specified product modules to the main target's Frameworks build phase
Optionally copies and links a GoogleService-Info.plist file to the resources phase
Automatically adds the -ObjC linker flag when Firebase products are detected
Configures Crashlytics run-script build phase and DWARF debug format when needed
Idempotent operation: skips packages and files already present in the project
limitations: Requires a macOS environment with Swift toolchain installed
Only works with standard Xcode project files (.xcodeproj), not raw workspaces
Cannot create a new Xcode project from scratch; an existing project must be present
Supports only a single native target (uses the first target found)

use_cases.title: Adding Firebase to a new iOS app
use_cases.description: Quickly integrate Firebase iOS SDK and its required GoogleService-Info.plist into an existing Xcode project without manual .pbxproj editing.
use_cases.target_user: iOS developers starting a new app

use_cases.title: Adding a third-party Swift package like Alamofire
use_cases.description: Install and link a non-Firebase Swift package by providing the repo URL, version, and product names to the tool.
use_cases.target_user: iOS developers adding networking or utility libraries

use_cases.title: Setting up CI-friendly dependency installation
use_cases.description: Use the script in automated pipelines to reproduce dependency setup deterministically across team members.

prompt_templates.title: Add Firebase Core to an iOS project
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Firebase to my iOS project. The project is at ./MyApp.xcodeproj and my GoogleService-Info.plist is at ./MyApp/GoogleService-Info.plist. Add FirebaseCore, FirebaseAuth, and FirebaseFirestore.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial Firebase setup for a new iOS app

prompt_templates.title: Add a generic Swift package
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the Alamofire Swift package to my Xcode project. Use the latest 5.x version from GitHub. The project is at ./MyApp.xcodeproj.

prompt_templates.title: Add Firebase with Crashlytics
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Firebase in my project including Crashlytics. My project is at ./MyApp.xcodeproj and config plist is at ./MyApp/GoogleService-Info.plist. Add FirebaseCore, FirebaseAnalytics, and FirebaseCrashlytics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full Firebase setup including crash reporting

prompt_templates.title: Verify and add specific product modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Xcode project at ./MyApp.xcodeproj and add the FirebaseDatabase product from firebase-ios-sdk at version 10.18.0. Confirm whether FirebaseCore is already linked.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user adding a specific module to an existing configuration

output_examples.input: Add FirebaseCore, FirebaseAuth to ./MyApp.xcodeproj with plist at ./GoogleService-Info.plist
output: Successfully added GoogleService-Info.plist to resources build phase.

output_examples.input: Re-run the same command on a project that already has FirebaseCore
Run on a directory with no Xcode project
output: No Xcode project found in this directory. Please create an empty Xcode project manually and let me know when you are ready to proceed.

best_practices: Always check the Firebase iOS SDK releases page for the latest version number before installing
Verify Swift is installed locally (swift --version) before invoking the skill
Ensure a GoogleService-Info.plist exists in your project before running the Firebase-specific command
Commit your .pbxproj changes to version control after running the tool so teammates can reproduce the setup
anti_patterns: Do not use Ruby, the xcodeproj gem, or sed to modify .pbxproj files manually
Do not skip the -ObjC linker flag when adding Firebase; it causes fatal runtime crashes
Do not write Swift source files directly into the .pbxproj file structure; use Xcode's folder synchronization instead
Do not run the script against a workspace that has no .xcodeproj file present

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: It runs a native Swift tool that adds Swift Package Manager dependencies to your Xcode project and links the specified product modules to your main target.

faq.question: Does it work on Linux or Windows?
faq.answer: No. It requires macOS with the Swift toolchain installed because it uses the XcodeProj library to manipulate .pbxproj files.

faq.question: Is the GoogleService-Info.plist required?
faq.answer: It is required only when you are adding Firebase products. For other packages like Alamofire, you can omit the --plist flag.

faq.question: What happens if I run it twice?
faq.answer: The script is idempotent. It detects packages and files that are already present in the project and skips them, so it is safe to re-run.

faq.question: Why must I avoid Ruby?
faq.answer: The skill mandates Swift instead of Ruby to prevent the well-known fragility of the xcodeproj Ruby gem. Ruby scripts can silently corrupt the .pbxproj file structure.

faq.question: Can it create a new Xcode project from scratch?
faq.answer: No. An existing .xcodeproj must be present. If none is found, the skill will ask you to create one in Xcode manually before continuing.
